title: Amazon Neptune Stream Record
description: Represents a change-log stream record from Neptune Streams. Each record captures a single mutation (ADD or REMOVE) made to graph data. Supports both property graph (PG_JSON format) and RDF (NQUADS format) records.

lastEventId.description: The sequence identifier of the last change in the response.

lastTrxTimestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp in milliseconds of the last transaction commit.

format.description: The serialization format of the stream records.

records.description: The array of change-log stream records.

totalRecords.description: The total number of records in the response.

EventId.title: Stream Event ID
EventId.description: A unique sequence identifier for a stream event, composed of the commit number and operation number.

opNum.description: The operation number within the commit.

PropertyGraphRecord.title: Property Graph Stream Record
PropertyGraphRecord.description: A change record for property graph (Gremlin/openCypher) data in PG_JSON format.

commitTimestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp in milliseconds of the transaction commit.

isLastOp.description: True only if this is the last operation in the transaction.

PropertyGraphData.title: Property Graph Data
PropertyGraphData.description: The property graph change data within a stream record.

id.description: The unique identifier of the graph element.

type.description: The type of graph element: v (vertex), vl (vertex label), vp (vertex property), e (edge), ep (edge property).

key.description: The property key name (for property changes).

from.description: The source vertex ID (for edge records only).

to.description: The target vertex ID (for edge records only).

SparqlRecord.title: SPARQL Stream Record
SparqlRecord.description: A change record for RDF (SPARQL) data in NQUADS format.

commitTimestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp in milliseconds of the transaction commit.

stmt.description: The N-Quads statement representing the RDF triple or quad change.

isLastOp.description: True only if this is the last operation in the transaction.
